creator: Alexander Terp
description:
RE-VERIFIED 2026-06-14 (rad v0.10.1): still reproduces (both forms error RAD10009; the `?? (-1)` workaround works).

Writing a negative number literal as the fallback value for ?? or catch causes a parse error.

**Repro:**
r = parse_int("x") ?? -1      // error[RAD10009]: Unexpected '-'
r = risky() catch -1          // error[RAD10009]: Unexpected '-'

**Workarounds:**
- Parentheses: `?? (-1)`  (confirmed working)
- A variable: `fallback = -1; r = parse_int("x") ?? fallback`
- Arithmetic: `?? (0 - 1)`

The parser does not recognize unary minus in this position. Natural to write - any user using ?? / catch with negative defaults hits it.

**Fix (slam dunk):** allow a unary-minus literal as the RHS of `??` / `catch` in the parser. The parens workaround proves it is purely a parsing gap.
title: ?? and catch cannot parse negative literal on right side
